mnemonics.title: Triple Bottom Line 3Ps
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'People need Planet-friendly Profits' - All three pillars must work together for true sustainability.

mnemonics.title: E-waste Hazards
mnemonics.content: Lead (L), Mercury (M), Cadmium (C), Chromium (C) - 'Lone Men Can't Crawl' when exposed to toxic e-waste.

mnemonics.title: SDGs Count
mnemonics.content: 17 SDGs = 'One Goal for Seven Billion' (roughly world population)

mnemonics.title: Green Computing Pillars
mnemonics.content: VREN: Virtualization, Recycling, Energy efficiency, New technologies - 'Very Respectful Engineers Notice' green computing importance.

acronyms.usage: Evaluating environmental impacts from raw material extraction to disposal
Business model integrating social and environmental concerns
Rating system for electronic product environmental performance
17 UN goals for global sustainable development by 2030
Standard for power management in computers
Gases contributing to global warming from IT operations

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a three-legged stool - all three legs (People, Planet, Profit) must be equal for the stool to stand. Remove one leg and it falls.
Think of PUE like efficiency in a car: lower number = less fuel wasted. Perfect PUE of 1.0 means all energy goes to computing, none to cooling/lights.
Linear = one-way street ending in a cliff (waste). Circular = roundabout (traffic) where materials keep moving and nothing is discarded.
Picture a product journey: Cradle (raw materials) → Manufacturing factory → Use phase (hands) → Grave/Rest (recycling or landfill). LCA follows entire path.

analogies.analogy: Like a bank account - we can only withdraw from nature at the rate we deposit back, or we'll leave debt for future generations.
Like making your home energy efficient - using LED bulbs, proper insulation, and smart thermostats reduces waste. Green computing does the same for IT.
Like carpooling - instead of one person per car (one server per task), multiple people share the ride (multiple VMs on one server), saving fuel/energy.
Like the water cycle - water evaporates, forms clouds, rains, flows back to rivers. Nothing is wasted, everything circulates.
